title: Make for Yourself a Teacher, Acquire for Yourself a Friend — The Power of Learning Relationships
title_zh: 为自己寻找老师,为自己结交学友——学习关系的力量
title_he: עֲשֵׂה לְךָ רַב וּקְנֵה לְךָ חָבֵר — כוחם של יחסי למידה
author: Rabbi Yehoshua ben Perachya
target_audience: Chinese speakers learning about Orthodox Judaism
key_concepts: Making oneself a teacher requires active pursuit and humility
Acquiring a learning friend requires investment and commitment
Teacher provides authority, experience, and structure
Friend provides equality, freedom, and mutual growth
Favorable judgment is the foundation for learning relationships
Jewish learning is relational, not solitary
practical_applications: How to find and approach a teacher
How to establish a regular study partnership
Creating a culture of favorable judgment
Building a complete learning ecosystem with both teacher and friend
talmudic_sources: Ta'anit 7a - 'Either study with a partner or die'
Stories of Rabbi Akiva and Rabbi Eliezer
seo_description_en: Understanding Pirkei Avot's wisdom on teacher-student relationships and study partnerships. Learn why Jewish tradition emphasizes both a teacher (rav) and a learning friend (chaver) for spiritual growth.
seo_description_zh: 理解《先贤之言》关于师生关系和学习伙伴的智慧。了解为什么犹太传统强调老师(rav)和学习朋友(chaver)对精神成长的重要性。
